s. f. Making love, amour, love, courtship, gallantry, the state of being a lover.

more: adj. compar. (of شامل) More or most comprehensive, perfect, complete, surpassing, transcendent.

def: adj. Acquainted (with, -se), knowing, known; attached (to), fond (of)
more: n. 1. Friend; companion; acquaintance; crony; intimate friend; bosom friend; familiar. 2. A lover; mate; paramour; sweetheart; cavalier servente; cicisbeo; flame; beau; amoroso. Ranḍī ke saikṛoṅ āshnā . Prov. A harlot's lovers may be reckoned by the hundred. 3. A kept mistress; inamorata ; concubine. Jin kī āshnā, unkī bagal meṅ . Prov. saying. 4. A woman's dog (a lover). 5. ( asnāo ). Rus. A son-in-law. āsh'nā , adj. 1. Acquainted; knowing. Maiṅ un se āshnā bhī nahīṅ . 2. Fond of; attached to; loving. Ranḍī paise kī āshnā . Prov. Money is the harlot's love. harf āsh'nā , n. An abecedarian; a freshwater sailor. dost āshnā, yār āshnā ; Rus. yār asnāo , n. m. Friends; relatives and kinsmen. sūrat āshnā , n. m. One known by sight only; a formal acquaintance.
s. m. f. An acquaintance, lover, friend. آشنا پرست āshnā-parast, Friendly, attentive to friends, sociable.
subs: adj. Friendly, attentive to friends, sociable, obliging
s.m.f. An apparent or outward friend
v.n. To become very friendly; to become tame or domesticated (an animal; syn. hil-jānā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آشْنَا (āšnā).

def: s.f. Acquaintance, friendship, intimacy, familiarity; connection, relationship; connection by marriage; illicit love, carnal intercourse
more: n. f. 1. Friendship; intimacy; familiarity; acquaintance; intercourse. 2. Relationship; connexion; relation through marriage. Mhārī unkī asnāī hai . Rus. 3. Carnal knowledge; illicit love; unlawful attachment. Dekho, bhaṛwe kī shāmat āī hai, Mujh se kahtā hai, "āshnāī hai ." Wom. āshnāī kar'nā, āshnāī joṛnā,—lagānā , v. a. 1. To form a friendship or intimacy; make friends with. Āshnāī karnī āsān, nibhānā mushkil . Prov. Friendships are soon made, but rarely ripened. 2. To form an unlawful attachment or connection. Rāt kisī ke saṅg tū jāgī, kar gaī chatrāī, Merī jān, is bāman ke saṅg karī āshnāī . Song. Lāonī. āshnāī lagnā, āshnāī honā , v. n. To become friends; to be united in friendship; to be fast friends.
s. f. Acquaintance, friendship. آشناٴي کرنا āshnā-ī-karnā, v. a. To associate, to be familiar, to unite. آشنائي لگنا āshnā-ī lagnā, v. n. To become intimate, to be united in friendship.
subs: āshnāʼī karnā (-se), To make acquaintance (with), make friends (with), strike up a friendship (with); to form an attachment or an intimacy (with); to form an unlawful attachment or connection (with)
āshnāʼī honā, v.n. To become friends (with), become intimate (with), to be united in friendship (with).

def: adv. In a friendly manner; through friendship; like a friend.
more: adv. In a friendly way, through friendship, like a friend.

Preux chevaliers ; honorable men. 3. In Rohilkhund, Oude, and Benares, a class of cultivators who claim certain privileges. ashrāf , adj. H. bhalā-mānas . 1 Gentle; meek; mild. 2. ety: said to be so called from Ashraf, the king in whose reign the coin obtained currency

s. f. A gold coin so called. (The Calcutta ashrafī is worth ₤1. 11 s. 8 d. and the gold of it is better than English standard gold by five shillings in the ounce, or about 1/16. By the regulations of May 1793, it should weigh 190.894 grains troy).

n. m. 1. A horse with a white face, tail, breast and hoofs. 2. A collection of eight lucky objects to be got together on occasion, as a lion, a bull, an elephant, a water-jar, a fan, a flag, a trumpet, and a lamp.

n. m. 1. The eight siddhis (perfect or superior beings). 2. A personification of the powers and laws of nature, when they are subjected to the will by holiness and austerities. Whatever the fancy desires, may, it is said, be obtained; universal sovereignty may be acquired, and implicit obedience to any command enforced; the magnitude, weight, or levity of the body may be increased or diminished at will, and the body be transported in an instant to any part of the universe.

Eight parts of man, viz. the hands, feet, thighs, breast, eyes, head, words and mind. اشٿانگ پرنام ash̤ṭāng-praṇām, s. m. Prostration, in salutation or adoration, so as to touch the ground with those eight parts.

def: n. m. S. अश्व a horse, मेधृ to kill. The actual or emblematic sacrifice of a horse, an ancient religious ceremony among the Hindūs. ☞ This sacrifice is one of the highest order, and its performance a hundred times, gives a title to the dominion of Swarga or paradise. It appears to have been originally typical, the horse and other animals being simply bound during the performance of certain ceremonies. The actual sacrifice is an introduction of a later period. Wilson.
more: s. m. The ceremony of sacrificing a horse.

A sluggard fell into a well. "Who's going to get up yet?" says he. Or thus, Askati, girā kuë meṅ, Kahā "hame ehaë bhalā ." Prov. A sluggard fell into a well, Quoth he, "I'll lie here just as well." 2. Rām nām ko askati, bhojan ko taīyār . Prov. Too slow on Ram to call, to eat too ready all. ās'katī , E. āl'kasī , W. al'hī , Gaṛh. adj. 1. Lazy; sluggish; inactive; doing nothing; slothful; dull; idle; torpid; slack; sluggish; remiss; indolent ( sust ). Phr. With folded arms; with the hands in the pockets. 2. Tardy; slow; late; behind hand; backward.

Truth; fact; reality. 1. Asal kah do, jhūṭ mat bolo . 2. Asal kahe so dārī-jār . E. Prov. Who speaks truth shall get abuse. 5. Lineage; genealogy; family ( kul ); stock; blood; birth; breed; extraction; race; line; descent; pedigree. 1. Us kī asal meṅ faraq hai . There is a stain in his pedigree. 2. Apnī asal pe ā gayā . Prov. His (low) birth has asserted itself. 6. A man of good family; a born gentleman. 1. Asal se khatā nahīṅ, kam-asal se wafā nahīṅ . Prov. Gentility has no flaw, the base-born no fidelity. 2. Asal ke asal hote haiṅ . Prov. Good s…

more: n. m. 1. The sky; the firmament; the heavens ( ākās ). 2. The celestial orb; the empyrean; welkin; the vault or canopy of heaven ( ambar ). Laṅgṛī kaṭṭo, asmān pe ghoṅslā . Prov. A lame squirrel, and her nest in the sky! (Said of small powers with great ambition). āsmān par thūk'nā , v. n. lit . To spit against the sky. To act improperly or foolishly. Āsmān kā thūkā, mūṅh par āve . Prov. Spit not against heaven. It will fall back on thy face. āsmān par chaṛhānā , v. a. 1. To elevate; exalt; exaggerate; speak Logoṅ ne tārīf kar-kar āsmān par chaṛhā dȳā . 2. To flatter; wheedle; coax ( phuslānā ). āsmān pe dimāg ho'nā , v. n. To be wise in one's own conceit, vain as a peacock, self-conceited, proud, etc. Naë nawāb, āsmān par dimāg . Prov. āsmān pe kheṅchnā, āsmān pe qadam rakhnā, āsmān meṅ pair lagānā , v. n. To exalt oneself; to grow vain or conceited; to affect greatness. Phr. To think …

(comp. of آس ās, A millstone, and مانند Like) s. m. The sky, the firmament, heaven, the celestial orb. آسمان پر قدم رکهنا āsmān par ḳadam rakhnā, To give oneʼs self airs, to aspire, to be vain. آسمان پر کهینچنا āsmān par khainchnā, To be proud, to affect greatness. آسمان سي گرنا āsmān se girnā, To fall from the clouds, is applied, particularly by women, to signify an acquisition that has been made suddenly and unexpectedly, without labour or care, and the value of which is inconsiderable. آسمان قدر āsmān-ḳadr, Vast as the firmament in size.

more: adj. 1. Heavenly; celestial; divine. 2. Azure; cerulean; sky-blue. Āsmānī raṅg . 3. Unexpected; abrupt; sudden. Āsmānī golā ān paṛā. āsmānī ba'lā,—thape'ṛā,—gaz'ab,—go'lā,— tīr , n. A calamity from Heaven; Divine visitation. Girī us pe jo āsmānī balā, Dil us nāznīṅ kā havā ho chalā . Mīr Hasan. āsmānī pilā'nā , v. a. To make one drunk, especially with bhang or toddy. ☞ The metaphor is taken from the feeling of the intoxicated person who seems to himself to be raised up aloft and descending again alternately. Hence the Arabic-Persian expression, falak-sair (Heaven-travel) for bhang and mājūm. āsmānī tīr , n. m. An arrow shot towards the sky; a useless act; an unprofitable action. āsmānī farmā'nī , E. n. f. lit . Heavenly decree. 1. Unforeseen disasters arising from drought or excessive rain, as failure of crops in a bad season. Sukhār dahār āsmānī farmānī hai . E. Drought and flood are He…
adj. Heavenly, celestial, from heaven, sky-coloured, cerulean, azure. آسماني تیروغیره āsmānī tīr wa g̠ẖairah, An arrow, &c. shot in the air.

def: s.f. pl. (of صنف ṣinf), Sorts, kinds, varieties.
s.m. Bathing, ablution, purification; (also written asnān)
more: n. m. S. स्ना , P. shinā to bathe. Bathing; ablution; purification. ashnān dhyān , n. m. Bathing and religious meditation; daily worship of the Hindūs. Kar ashnān, dhyān rakh dil meṅ, Mandar Shiv kā jā kholā, Dhūp, dīp, naībed chaṛhā-kar pānī meṅ kum kum gholā. Bhajan . Hymn. ritu asnān ; Hin. Wom. mahīne kā nahān , n. Bathing after menstruation. Gaṅgā ashnān , n. Bathing in the Ganges.
subs: s.m. Bathing and religious meditation, daily worship (of a Hindū).

def: s.m. Bathing.
wety: From Sanskrit स्नान (snāna).

def: s.m. Want of affection, unkindness; — adj. Wanting in affection, unkind, harsh.
